125286.explanation: We believe residents should be directly involved in important decisions. A referendum is a powerful tool to make the voice of Veendam residents heard and to strengthen local democracy. This creates more support and better decisions.

125287.explanation: We encourage citizen initiatives but want to be sure that management and quality are secured in the long term. Many residents appreciate when the municipality takes responsibility. Therefore, the municipality remains primarily responsible, with room for those who want to contribute themselves.

125290.explanation: We promote sustainable grassroots initiatives. When residents generate energy together, it benefits both the environment and their wallets. The municipality assists with advice, energy coaches, and subsidies, but no extra money: residents should not have to pay for neighbors’ improvements through municipal taxes.

125292.explanation: GemeenteBelangen believes Veendam’s capacity must be leading. The asylum seekers center is a national task with local impact. The shelter is temporary and should remain so. We want a rapid, humane phase-out when possible and keep municipal control.

125293.explanation: GemeenteBelangen looks at quality and suitability, not gender. The best people must be in the right positions to advance Veendam. A strict quota is symbolic politics and undermines the best candidate. We are mindful of group composition but prioritize the best candidate.

125302.explanation: Sports and exercise are essential for health and social cohesion. We believe everyone, regardless of income, should be able to participate in sports activities. Paying membership fees for people on welfare is an investment in wellbeing and prevents social exclusion.

125303.explanation: A strong local broadcaster can make an important contribution to information provision in our municipality. Additional financial support from the municipality is possible if it results in clear extra added value.

125306.explanation: If a club faces financial difficulties, we offer help and support to develop good plans together. Sports clubs are the backbone of our community. The municipality can guarantee loans to help clubs become more sustainable and reduce costs.

125308.explanation: Safety and livability are top priorities for GemeenteBelangen. More enforcement officers provide more supervision, less nuisance, and a safer feeling in neighborhoods and villages. BOA’s show hospitality and intervene only when truly necessary: a concrete investment in quality of life.

125309.explanation: Camera surveillance can help increase safety and detect crime where needed. We support camera surveillance at strategic locations, provided it is done carefully and residents' privacy is safeguarded.

125312.explanation: We believe our own residents should have priority in allocating social rental housing. This is a matter of local interest and ensures that Veendam residents who need housing actually get a chance in their own municipality.

125316.explanation: We believe everyone should contribute to society according to their ability. A reciprocal obligation for welfare benefits through socially meaningful activities helps activate and re-integrate individuals. This ensures a fair distribution of our shared responsibilities.

125318.explanation: Poverty is a persistent problem. Everyone, regardless of income, should be eligible for help when needed. This allows the municipality to support more effectively where the need is greatest and provide people with appropriate assistance.

125320.explanation: The municipality should facilitate this by providing sufficient charging points upon request and at logical locations as established in the ordinance. This enables residents and visitors to charge their electric cars properly without unnecessarily reducing other parking spaces.

125321.explanation: We believe the speed limit should be assessed per situation. In some places, 30 km/h can increase safety, but elsewhere 50 km/h is fine. A general reduction is not always necessary and can hinder traffic flow. Customization is important; this is also stated in the current mobility plan.

125322.explanation: We believe everyone has the right to care that closely matches personal needs. The municipality must ensure sufficient diversity in the care supply.

125323.explanation: The quality of youth care is of utmost importance. Cutting youth care by focusing solely on price can lead to worse care and larger problems in the long term. We choose quality and effectiveness, not the lowest cost.

125324.explanation: We believe the burden on residents and entrepreneurs should be kept as low as possible. We advocate a stable property tax level, with at most an inflation correction. If budget space arises, targeted reductions in property tax can be considered to further support the local economy.

125327.explanation: We want to stimulate the local economy and create employment. Sufficient space in business parks is needed to attract new companies and allow existing ones to grow. This must be done carefully and suitably within the environment. Specifically, we support this with the Dallen III business park.

125329.explanation: We believe all housing seekers, especially Veendam residents, should have a fair chance at social rental housing. Priority for specific groups, such as refugees, comes at the expense of others and can lead to dissatisfaction. We advocate fair, transparent allocation.

125330.explanation: Free disposal of waste at the recycling center helps responsible separation and prevents dumping. However, this results in higher waste charges, which must be carefully weighed. With a Veendam pass, we can regulate this usage and specifically target our own residents.

125335.explanation: We believe new construction should fit the character and scale of Veendam. High-rise apartment buildings often do not match the existing streetscape and can harm livability. We choose appropriate housing that aligns with the surroundings.

125336.explanation: Subdivision of homes can lead to nuisance, parking problems, and reduced livability. In some cities it provides relief during housing shortages, but this is not currently the case in Veendam. We want to preserve the quality of our residential neighborhoods and approach this cautiously, enforcing existing rules.

125338.explanation: A car-free center can harm accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs. We believe the center must remain easily accessible, both for cars and for cyclists and pedestrians. We seek a balance between vibrancy and accessibility.

125339.explanation: A clean living environment is important. In all neighborhoods, including Veendam-Noord, waste must be collected in an appropriate and sufficiently frequent manner. Where it appears that the current collection is insufficient or causes nuisance, the municipality must adjust its collection policy accordingly.

125340.explanation: Subsidence caused by salt extraction has direct consequences for our residents and infrastructure. We believe the municipality must play an active role in this and make clear agreements with the government to protect Veendam’s interests.

125341.explanation: Reactivating Wildervank train station improves accessibility to Wildervank and Veendam, stimulates the local economy, and strengthens public transport. This should include good connections with a public transport hub (bus). This increases the mobility of all our residents.

125342.explanation: Independent research is the basis for GB’s decisions regarding the living environment. Recently, independent scientific research was conducted in Veendam by the GGD, Province, and RIVM. Conclusion: air quality meets all standards. Follow-up research should be possible if new facts or concerns arise.

125286.explanation: Active citizenship strengthens democracy. Policy works better when developed together with residents. A referendum can be a useful tool in this, but other forms can also increase support for decisions.

125287.explanation: A strong government is important to maintain public facilities such as a sports hall for everyone. A public park is also such a facility that must be maintained by the government. Transfer to the private sector should only happen in very exceptional cases.

125290.explanation: The energy transition is a challenge in which we, as government and residents, have a serious role. Citizen initiatives deserve support from the municipality. The proceeds gained should be reinvested locally and benefit the community itself.

125292.explanation: The asylum problem is largely self-created by the national government. As a municipality, we must stand for a solidarity-based, humane part of the solution. We prefer small-scale, more structural asylum reception.

125293.explanation: Agriculture and horticulture also face labor shortages and often have to rely on seasonal workers from abroad. A decent employer must provide not only a fair wage but also decent housing, preferably on their own premises.

125302.explanation: Sports are important for everyone to stay fit and feel good. People with limited means must be given the same opportunities by the municipality. Fortunately, this is already reasonably well organized in Veendam.

125303.explanation: A local broadcaster such as RTV1 deserves structural support from the municipality. Such a facility is crucial for the functioning of our local democracy.

125306.explanation: Sports clubs in Veendam are important for promoting health and social cohesion. The municipality supports the clubs in maintenance and providing grounds. Where there are issues, consultation is needed. Possibly the municipality could contribute extra.

125308.explanation: The number of enforcement officers (boa’s) in Veendam is sufficient. It is important, however, that public order and safety continue to receive enough attention and resources, especially concerning the risks of undermining crime.

125309.explanation: There is sufficient camera surveillance; personal supervision by police and enforcement officers works better.

125312.explanation: The right to a rental home applies to everyone in the Netherlands. Although it seems sympathetic to give priority to local residents, we must also be and remain solidary in this.

125316.explanation: Receiving social assistance benefits is not a favor but reflects a decent welfare state that must be preserved and strengthened. Where people need help toward meaningful work, we must fully invest in that.

125318.explanation: Help for those who cannot make ends meet must be accessible to everyone. People with multiple jobs often struggle to get by and do not always know what they are entitled to. The municipality has a role here.

125320.explanation: More and more cars are electric and need charging stations. The municipality must take responsibility for this but also not lose sight of cyclists and pedestrians.

125321.explanation: Many streets and roads in Veendam have already been designated as 30-kilometer zones. This is a good thing, and where possible, it should be expanded. Enforcement of the speed limit deserves more attention.

125322.explanation: Needing care is often difficult and affects your autonomy. Therefore, it is important to have a choice of the care provider that suits you.

125323.explanation: The quality of care must be leading. Cost savings are mainly found in how internal and external collaboration occurs.

125327.explanation: The number of business parks in Veendam is large, and there are still many opportunities for new companies to establish themselves. Continuing attention to retailers and both large and small companies is very important for a strong economic Veendam.

125329.explanation: Refugees with residence status have the same rights and obligations as other Dutch people. On the housing market, they need an advantage to actually be able to build their lives.

125330.explanation: Waste collection has long not been free and cannot be. We produce a lot of waste together, the processing of which also costs a lot of money. For green waste, delivering and composting is actually profitable. This should therefore remain free.

125335.explanation: There are already many apartment buildings or towers in Veendam. Building residential courtyards or neighborhoods promotes mutual cohesion and looking out for each other.

125336.explanation: Room rental is interesting for a limited group in Veendam. Renting out houses or studio apartments is clearly preferred, provided rental prices remain decent and especially affordable.

125338.explanation: The center of Veendam certainly needs a boost. The center should be greener and designed as a place to stay. Cars do not fit into this and must be parked on the edges. Pedestrians and cyclists have priority.

125339.explanation: The frequency of waste collection must be tailored to the amount of waste generated weekly in a neighborhood. Litter must be actively combated and cleaned up. All neighborhoods must have sufficient trash bins.

125340.explanation: Veendam has layered mining which poses a serious problem for subsidence. The mining companies are responsible here, and the State Supervision of Mines supervises this. Veendam must not become an exploitation area and must cooperate with the Ministry on this.

125341.explanation: With the arrival of an isotope factory, the De Dallen industrial area can get a boost. This also deserves a full train station nearby as part of the Nedersaksen line. The municipality must strongly advocate for this.

125342.explanation: Ongoing research must be conducted to monitor safety for residents.

125286.explanation: The SP has submitted a national referendum law, giving the population the right to call out politics.

125287.explanation: Residents have too little say over their neighborhood/district and municipality.

125290.explanation: The subsidy should have been used.

125292.explanation: We must continue to support people who truly need help.

125303.explanation: Watchdog of local democracy: They monitor local government and the municipal council, which is crucial for transparency in the municipality.

125306.explanation: This depends on the situation. If it solves something temporarily, then we find it fine, but not permanently.

125308.explanation: There have been too many budget cuts to the police, which has reduced safety, supervision, and enforcement. Expansion is necessary.

125309.explanation: In certain situations such as the fair, camera surveillance is needed. More camera surveillance does not solve the problem; there must be more police presence on the streets.

125312.explanation: Agreed, otherwise they will leave our municipality.

125316.explanation: People on welfare benefits can become lonely, and volunteer work helps with that. Moreover, we believe in principle that something should be expected in return for welfare benefits.

125318.explanation: The threshold for poverty schemes up to, for example, 150% of the minimum wage.

125320.explanation: There are currently enough in Veendam.

125321.explanation: Placing only 30 km/h signs does not promote traffic safety. The infrastructure must also be adjusted.

125322.explanation: This can only be done if it is possible.

125323.explanation: Must pay attention to what fits here, tailored solutions.

125324.explanation: The municipality does not have enough from the incoming money alone, but also needs these contributions.

125329.explanation: Just like other home seekers on a waiting list.

125330.explanation: Our municipality also needs to generate income. However, we favor sometimes making it free. This way we hope to prevent littering.

125335.explanation: High residential towers do not fit the image of Veendam.

125336.explanation: Landlords who exploit tenants will benefit from this.

125338.explanation: At this moment it is considered unsafe in the shopping street. In the evening, it is used as a racetrack.

125339.explanation: If there are problems here, they must first be mapped out.

125340.explanation: It must also be ensured that soil subsidence is not exceeded.

125341.explanation: The Veendam train station is 3 km away, and the roads are not arranged in such a way that there is room for a train station.

125342.explanation: No expansion of heavy or hazardous industry near residential neighborhoods.

125286.explanation: We are in favor of people being able to express their opinion, whether through a referendum or by organizing citizen meetings.

125287.explanation: When they can make good agreements with the municipality about this and fulfill them, more responsibilities may be placed on the residents. It's about having heart for the cause and, for example, prioritizing the importance of sports facilities. Small leftover corners or green strips can be perfectly transferred.

125290.explanation: This is really important and the subsidies should also go to residents and not, as so often, to large companies and project developers. Our motto is: together and for each other.

125292.explanation: Safety for and of people remains important. It is clear that refugees need a temporary place. We must also help these people.

125293.explanation: Let them do that, and ensure that the housing is decent so that these people can live there comfortably and well.

125302.explanation: Certainly, the Stadjers Pass in Groningen is a good example of this.

125303.explanation: We have been supporting the local broadcaster financially for years. We must continue doing this.

125306.explanation: Sports clubs are already helped by the municipality with housing, maintenance, etc. Further support is only possible in very exceptional situations.

125308.explanation: Our municipality has a well-equipped group of enforcement officers. In cooperation with surrounding municipalities, we can also handle special situations.

125309.explanation: We only use camera surveillance where necessary.

125312.explanation: Many people in our municipality are hard looking for social housing. This is really a problem we want to solve.

125316.explanation: Only for people who want and can do this.

125318.explanation: More people need to be helped. And you must ensure poverty prevention.

125320.explanation: People with an electric car can install a facility for this at their home.

125321.explanation: But do fine people who drive too fast. So more enforcement.

125322.explanation: Care providers are hard to find. Expressing preferences within this is possible but must then be paid for by the people themselves, for example, on a self-employed basis. Furthermore, discrimination against care providers is completely unacceptable to us. We live together.

125323.explanation: Price is important alongside quality. But not everything can be paid for by the municipality, so more attention must and may be paid to the price.

125324.explanation: But definitely not increase. It has risen enough in recent years.

125327.explanation: In principle, there are enough business parks in our opinion. In the future, we will look again to see if it is necessary and appropriate.

125329.explanation: Everyone has the right to a social rental home. For this, there is an appropriate points system.

125330.explanation: This is free with us, and there are sufficient options to dispose of chemical waste. Entrepreneurs who produce such waste should definitely have to pay for this.

125335.explanation: Tall residential towers do not belong in our municipality.

125336.explanation: Landlords are allowed to rent out individual rooms. As a municipality, we have little influence on this. What we especially need to achieve is sufficient affordable rental housing, particularly for young people.

125338.explanation: Cars belong in a municipality of our size. Certainly also to maintain a livable center.

125339.explanation: It perhaps should even be free. A lot of waste is dumped along the roadside. So it is good to collect the waste.

125340.explanation: We are strong proponents of this. Our party wants to stop salt mining. The benefits do not in any way compare meaningfully to the disadvantages, such as soil subsidence, soils becoming salty, insufficient drinking water, etcetera.

125341.explanation: Only if this is found to add value after investigation. The Nedersaksen line can be crucial for this.

125342.explanation: Certainly. Continuous measurement and research must be done on the effects of such industry in Veendam.

125286.explanation: The council is an elected body with a mandate.

125290.explanation: We are not in favor of subsidy flows.

125292.explanation: We will continue to enforce the law.

125293.explanation: We do not want to make this compulsory. Where possible, we will encourage it.

125302.explanation: The Leergeld foundation, in cooperation with the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture, already takes care of this.

125306.explanation: No, associations remain financially responsible themselves.

125312.explanation: We also consider economic ties a factor.

125316.explanation: A reciprocal obligation is possible if physical and/or mental condition allows.

125320.explanation: There have been sufficient ones so far.

125321.explanation: We do want this in residential neighborhoods.

125324.explanation: Veendam should look at its own costs.

125327.explanation: As soon as there is demand.

125335.explanation: Not higher than the tallest building currently.

125341.explanation: Good accessibility for residents is not a luxury but a necessity.

125286.explanation: D66 supports more direct democracy. A local referendum is a good instrument as a supplement to democracy via the municipal council, provided it concerns subjects suitable for a yes/no question and sufficient information is available for residents.

125287.explanation: D66 encourages the own initiative of residents and organizations. If residents want this themselves and good agreements are made about costs, safety, accessibility, and continuity, D66 is in favor. The municipality supervises and remains closely involved.

125290.explanation: Generating sustainable energy is important for the energy transition. The municipality should assist residents where possible and targeted with advice, subsidies, guarantees, and loans.

125292.explanation: D66 believes there must be room for people fleeing war and violence. Veendam, like every municipality, should do its part. The reception center on Lloydsweg is operating smoothly. In Veendam, there is sufficient space for small-scale asylum reception.

125293.explanation: D66 thinks employers are responsible for decent housing for seasonal workers, but this does not necessarily have to be on their own premises.

125302.explanation: D66 wants to definitely maintain the fund for sports and culture for adults. No one should be sidelined due to lack of money.

125303.explanation: An independent press is essential in a democracy. D66 warmly supports the local broadcaster.

125306.explanation: D66 sees sports clubs as the backbone of the local community. In cases of temporary or exceptional problems, the municipality should help (but no open-ended funding). The municipality can also assist through advice or guarantees, for example.

125308.explanation: More deployment and visibility of enforcement officers can help. Good cooperation with the police and increased police presence in our region is more important.

125309.explanation: D66 supports camera surveillance when there are demonstrable safety issues, if it is a temporary situation, and if the privacy of Veendam residents and visitors is well protected.

125312.explanation: Housing shortages do not stop at our municipal borders. Within the legal frameworks, there may be room to prioritize people with local ties, but structural priority for local residents that leaves people from other municipalities with no chance at all is not supported by D66.

125316.explanation: D66 is in favor of encouraging people to participate in society and helping them find (volunteer) work. D66 wants to assist people with tailored solutions and perspective. Coercion usually has the opposite effect.

125318.explanation: Help must always fit the reality of people struggling to make ends meet. Many people just miss out on various arrangements, and support must also be accessible for working people with low incomes. In the municipality of Veendam, however, the income limit is already relatively high.

125320.explanation: Charging infrastructure is necessary for the switch to cleaner transport. Since there are more electric cars, we also need more charging points.

125321.explanation: D66 wants to introduce a 30 km/h speed limit on as many roads as possible because this is good for traffic safety. However, on through roads (where buses also travel), this is usually not feasible.

125322.explanation: D66 finds personal choice important, and this also applies in healthcare. However, this should not lead to discrimination against groups, and 'always' is probably not feasible.

125323.explanation: The quality of the care provided is important. Veendam is on the right track with the JEP, but D66 is critical of the freedom of choice still available to those seeking help.

125324.explanation: D66 is in favor of a balanced tax policy. We cannot support lowering the property tax if it would jeopardize important facilities or investments.

125327.explanation: D66 wants a smart, green, and future-proof economy and sees potential for this in Veendam, for example in the health science sector.

125329.explanation: Veendam must house the number of recognized refugees determined by the national government. This must be decent housing and done in a way that is fair to everyone. This sometimes requires granting priority, but if it can also be done in another way, that is preferred.

125330.explanation: D66 finds good waste separation important, but this proposal is financially unfeasible. D66 prefers (financial) incentives to encourage proper waste separation and recycling.

125335.explanation: It depends on exactly how tall those apartment buildings are, but very tall buildings do deviate from the current image of Veendam. In general, high-rise buildings are acceptable if they solve housing shortages and fit within a sustainable and livable neighborhood.

125336.explanation: D66 believes flexible housing market solutions can be used to keep housing affordable, provided the quality and living conditions remain adequate.

125338.explanation: D66 promotes sustainable mobility and a clean living environment. A completely car-free center in Veendam is not feasible and also not desirable. However, D66 would like to see Kerkstraat and Museumplein become greener and car-free.

125339.explanation: The frequency of waste collection must be sustainable and financially feasible. More frequent collection is not always desirable.

125340.explanation: D66 thinks the municipality must actively ensure protection of the living environment and safety of residents. Agreements with the national government about the effects of salt mining are part of careful governance and limit risks for houses, infrastructure, and the environment.

125341.explanation: D66 is not against a second station, but considers this financially unfeasible. D66 believes that the municipality of Veendam should fully commit to the Nedersaksenlijn and strive for good bus connections and cycling routes within Veendam.

125342.explanation: This study has already been conducted and concluded that the residents of Veendam have not been exposed to any significant (additional) risk. The municipality must do more to promote clean air, for example by joining the Clean Air Agreement.

125286.explanation: As a party, we want to avoid a referendum by being close to the people and involving them beforehand.

125287.explanation: Citizen participation is good, but assigned management must remain a municipal responsibility to prevent neglect.

125290.explanation: There are sufficient subsidies and other financial resources available for sustainability outside the municipality. The municipality should play an advisory role.

125292.explanation: As a municipality, we have the responsibility to care for all residents, including new residents.

125293.explanation: Not necessarily on their own business premises, but responsible for promoting good living conditions.

125302.explanation: This should not be a standard. Tailored solutions are needed.

125303.explanation: A local broadcaster is important in the municipality for sharing news and information.

125308.explanation: Given the increase in aggression and vandalism, expansion is necessary.

125309.explanation: There are mobile cameras that can be deployed. Additional purchases require tailored solutions.

125312.explanation: To ensure that Veendam residents remain Veendam residents, we prefer allocation with priority for the Veendam residents.

125318.explanation: This is sufficiently arranged in Veendam. However, it is questionable whether the legal minimum is still adequate.

125320.explanation: Provided that the number of non-electric parking spaces remains the same.

125322.explanation: This will be asked of those in need of care, provided the supply is sufficient.

125323.explanation: Attention is paid to the price. To prevent unnecessary market forces, we have the Youth Expertise Plan.

125324.explanation: A decrease means that the municipality's revenues will increase elsewhere.

125327.explanation: Currently there is still sufficient capacity.

125330.explanation: Free means that the costs are charged to citizens in another way.

125335.explanation: That is allowed, but not higher than the tower of the PKN Church.

125336.explanation: We are not a student city.

125338.explanation: Where it is unsafe and/or disturbing, this will be addressed.

125339.explanation: Waste collection is uniform throughout Veendam.

125340.explanation: There are agreements and enforcement is necessary.

125341.explanation: This is a desire of the CDA.

125342.explanation: This has already been implemented. Continuous monitoring remains essential.

125286.explanation: Referenda are not a solution for political issues and are not binding. A broad survey is a better instrument to hear residents on a specific topic. The local government makes a well-considered decision and remains ultimately responsible.

125287.explanation: Being jointly responsible, for example for one's own public space in the street or village and/or facilities, stimulates ownership and community spirit. Actively promote the 'Right to challenge'.

125290.explanation: The municipality can support the permit application and manpower and possibly designate or find a location.

125292.explanation: We take our responsibility and strive for a permanent small-scale shelter location. The current shelter does not cause significant nuisance.

125293.explanation: Housing only on own property when it meets the set criteria. Control and enforcement are a must.

125302.explanation: The Learning and Sports Park and Borgerswold offer opportunities to exercise, and low-income individuals can apply to the Sports and Culture Fund.

125303.explanation: Government funding must be sufficient. The municipality also receives less from the national government and must spend its budget efficiently and thoughtfully.

125306.explanation: Only for support in facilitating or searching for accommodation, but not regarding too few members or income.

125308.explanation: BOAs play an important role; they are the eyes and ears for the district officer. They supervise and contribute to public safety.

125309.explanation: Only in case of nuisance, for a certain period, and with the aim of preventing crime.

125312.explanation: The urgency measure may be reintroduced for truly urgent situations.

125316.explanation: But it always remains tailored; volunteer work must not obstruct finding day activities or paid employment.

125318.explanation: Poverty has a large impact on the entire life; asking for help also offers opportunities to combat poverty. Annually review the poverty pact through conversations with residents and experts by experience.

125320.explanation: Match supply and demand with providers from the commercial sector.

125321.explanation: Set the roads in residential areas to 30 km/h and design them so that driving 30 km/h is self-evident. On main roads, maintain 50 km/h when possible, often combined with safe bicycle paths.

125322.explanation: The care provider must agree with the municipality in the contract that the care recipient is treated with respect for their own faith, culture, or beliefs.

125323.explanation: The quality of youth care must be leading; control over expenses is a must, no blank checks.

125324.explanation: The property tax gives the municipality financial room to realize facilities and developments but must be very cautious about increasing it. The property tax is not a cash cow or gap filler.

125327.explanation: On the business parks Dallen II and Veendammer Poort, parcels are still available and offer enough space for the coming years.

125329.explanation: We adhere to the quotas imposed on the municipality by the national government and strive for appropriate housing for status holders as well as for our current residents.

125330.explanation: In addition to the current contribution for deposits, we also propose three free waste collection days, combined with a rotating pickup route.

125335.explanation: What is high? Adjacent to the buildings, 6 to 8 residential floors is the max; taller tower blocks do not fit the image of Veendam.

125336.explanation: Provided shared use of shower, toilet, and kitchen facilities. Control of safety, smoke detectors, and fire extinguishing equipment.

125338.explanation: Yes, speed limit of 30 km/h and sufficient parking.

125339.explanation: Then other neighborhoods too, focus more on managing waste streams and collect different containers once every three weeks and possibly offer extra containers for large gardens and large families. Waste disposal fees increase more often and thus become more expensive.

125340.explanation: The maximum soil subsidence is already a fact. Residents must be generously compensated from the damage fund without proof and complicated procedures. Magnesium and salt extraction must not become a second gas dossier.

125341.explanation: Depends on the continuation of the Nedersaksen line. This train does not want extra stops due to time savings. If the Nedersaksen line does not proceed, Veendam can choose to create the stop in Wildervank with Arriva.

125342.explanation: Measuring is knowing; the emission of lead is always harmful to humans and animals. The research conducted is too much based on assumptions, tables, and calculations.

125286.explanation: DURF supports referenda. Referenda not only strengthen democracy but also provide an opportunity to restore the trust between citizens and the government.

125287.explanation: DURF encourages this kind of initiative and believes the municipality should facilitate it where necessary. It increases social cohesion. The government should also trust citizens to take responsibility.

125290.explanation: DURF grants everyone the freedom to develop such initiatives. But spending tax money on something private parties can just as well do is wasteful of public funds. There are more important matters for the people of Veendam.

125292.explanation: We definitely do not want new shelter locations. Extension of existing sites will be considered on a case-by-case basis, as long as the interests of residents and the municipality are safeguarded.

125293.explanation: If employers cannot attract local workers and therefore need seasonal workers, these companies must arrange housing themselves. That responsibility lies with them, not with the community.

125302.explanation: DURF believes taxes should be lowered so that everyone can afford membership of a sports club. The municipality should strive to create opportunities in good consultation, for example by helping people organize associations themselves.

125303.explanation: No, that is not a task of the municipality. If there is demand for a local broadcaster, they will earn enough from advertisements to support themselves.

125306.explanation: The municipality should facilitate this and can, for example, offer an affordable loan. But the community does not need to pay for minority interests.

125308.explanation: They should be better trained and better deployed. We simply want a police station back.

125309.explanation: At DURF, we are fundamentally opposed to the use of (mass) surveillance techniques that violate public privacy without proven effectiveness.

125312.explanation: Whoever has been on the list the longest has the greatest right to a home.

125316.explanation: At DURF, we oppose coercion; moreover, it disrupts the labor market. You must not force vulnerable people to do the dirty work.

125318.explanation: That is reasonable because inflation is so high and seems likely to remain so. Even people with relatively high incomes are now facing difficulties.

125320.explanation: Why should the municipality take care of this? Considering grid congestion, is it even possible to realize such connections now? This is a private matter that electric vehicle users can solve themselves.

125321.explanation: There are already more than enough restrictions, rules, and mandates for car traffic. Do not make it impossible for motorists.

125322.explanation: Freedom of choice should be offered. As long as the taxpayer is not worse off, we see no problem with this.

125323.explanation: Yes, of course. But definitely also on quality. It is about the price-quality ratio.

125324.explanation: Given the sky-high tax burden, it is only fair that the municipality ensures local charges remain as low as possible.

125327.explanation: That must be decided on a case-by-case basis. Employment is important and business activity is good, but we do not want even more polluting or environmentally harmful industry.

125329.explanation: DURF believes that everyone has equal rights. No one has more rights than another.

125330.explanation: That is already the case in many municipalities. There must be no problem with litter.

125335.explanation: Densification, i.e., high-rise buildings, is a good way to address the housing shortage. However, they must not disrupt the village appearance.

125336.explanation: DURF believes homeowners should be able to rent rooms to students and other house seekers without municipal intervention. People should be free to do what they want with their own homes.

125338.explanation: Not everyone can travel by public transport or bike! Entrepreneurs, residents, and visitors must be able to access the center by car, without distinctions based on car type or time of day.

125339.explanation: Of course, residents pay for this. It is one of the core tasks of the municipality.

125340.explanation: The party causing the damage must pay for it. Property rights must be protected by the government. Agreements about how many centimeters do not make a difference here. The key point is that damage is compensated by the perpetrator.

125341.explanation: DURF believes better public transport is essential for the economy, business climate, and accessibility of Veendam and its surroundings.

125342.explanation: Keep monitoring, with the health of Veendam residents as the priority.

125286.explanation: We are not against a referendum but believe more in good citizen participation beforehand. Therefore, we want each council proposal to clearly state how residents have been involved and what has been done with their input.

125287.explanation: The management of public facilities is the responsibility of the municipality. Volunteers and neighborhood initiatives are very valuable; their involvement strengthens liveability and should be actively encouraged by the municipality.

125290.explanation: We support residents who want to generate sustainable energy together. Especially through pilots where neighbors or neighborhoods share solar energy, the municipality can help to get this off the ground practically and affordably.

125292.explanation: We are against closure. The Spread Act calls for fair distribution of shelter. Refugees from war and violence deserve protection. Veendam has shown for two years that shelter can be done well. In case of nuisance, strict and consistent enforcement is required.

125293.explanation: We oppose this. Too often there is labor and housing exploitation, sometimes even modern slavery. Hard action must be taken against this: not only fines but also closure of businesses where necessary.

125302.explanation: We are in favor. Sport is important for health, self-confidence, and social contacts. Everyone should be able to participate, even with a low income. Therefore, the municipality must support sports participation for people on social assistance.

125303.explanation: We support extra funding for the local broadcaster. Local media provide news from Veendam itself: about the council, neighborhoods, and associations. Without sufficient support, this close-to-home information disappears and residents know less about local developments.

125306.explanation: We support assistance, but the principle is that sports clubs must be able to exist independently. If clubs face temporary difficulties, the municipality must be able to provide tailored support to maintain their social function.

125308.explanation: We are in favor of more enforcement officers. Good cooperation between community enforcement officers and police is essential. This way, officers can focus on supervision and enforcement in neighborhoods, and police can focus more on their core tasks.

125309.explanation: We oppose permanently increasing camera surveillance. In Veendam, it is already deployed when there is cause. Temporary extra supervision may be necessary during incidents or events, but we do not consider permanent expansion desirable.

125312.explanation: We are in favor. When allocating social rental homes, social or economic ties with Veendam should weigh more heavily. This way, people who live, work, or grew up here have a better chance to continue living in their own municipality.

125316.explanation: We are against a mandatory reciprocal obligation. People often end up on social assistance unexpectedly due to illness, dismissal, or other setbacks. Coercion does not help in such cases. The municipality should support, guide, and assist in finding suitable work where possible.

125318.explanation: We are against raising the income threshold. The current limit of 120% of the social minimum is carefully chosen and matches those who truly need it. More important than expanding it is providing good customized solutions and access to existing schemes.

125320.explanation: We are in favor. There are currently too few charging points in Veendam, while the number of electric cars is growing. The municipality must anticipate this by creating more public charging points, distributed across the neighborhoods.

125321.explanation: We are against a general 30 km/h standard. Main roads should remain at 50 km/h. Where 30 km/h is necessary, such as near schools, crossings, and shops, the road should also be designed to feel safe and logical.

125322.explanation: We are in favor. Within the municipality's influence, care should be tailored to who the person is. When procuring care, there must be room for freedom of choice, so people can receive care that fits their faith, culture, or worldview.

125323.explanation: We are against selection based mainly on price. Veendam demonstrates with the Youth Expertise Point (JEP) that working with fewer providers and more cooperation improves quality, controls costs better, reduces fragmentation, and delivers faster help for children and families.

125324.explanation: We do not turn this into symbolic politics. For most residents, the property tax (OZB) involves an increase of only a few tens of euros per year. We value honesty more than raising expectations the municipality cannot fulfill, so facilities in Veendam remain adequate.

125327.explanation: We are in favor. Additional business parks contribute to employment, strengthen the entrepreneurial climate, and enable targeted investments in infrastructure. This is important for a vital and future-proof economy in Veendam.

125329.explanation: We are in favor, within the municipality’s legal task. We understand it is frustrating when people have to wait for a home. The average waiting time in Veendam is about one year, affecting a few dozen homes annually. The real problem is the housing shortage, not who lives there.

125330.explanation: We are in favor. Residents should be able to dispose of their waste easily. Allowing four times a year free waste drop-off at the environmental station is clear, fair, and prevents illegal dumping and litter in neighborhoods.

125335.explanation: We are in favor. To reduce the housing shortage, building upwards is sometimes necessary. We adhere to the current standard: medium to low-rise buildings that fit Veendam’s scale and primarily in suitable locations such as the center.

125336.explanation: We are against structural house splitting. Dividing homes for quick short-term profit often harms livability, peace, and cohesion in the neighborhood. This mainly negatively impacts communities. Renting a room within one’s own home is not included here.

125338.explanation: We are against a car-free center. In Veendam, cars are necessary for many residents and visitors. The center is easily accessible and has sufficient parking spaces. This is a strength we should preserve, not limit.

125339.explanation: We are in favor of more frequent waste collection in Veendam-Noord. Residual waste is currently collected only once every four weeks, which is too infrequent for many households. More frequent collection, for example every two weeks, prevents nuisance and better matches practice.

125340.explanation: We are in favor. Residents are concerned about soil subsidence and safety. Veendam must therefore make clear agreements with the national government about limits to soil subsidence. The municipality should gain more influence towards Nedmag so residents' interests are better protected.

125341.explanation: We are in favor. Reopening Wildervank station aligns with the development of Dallen II and III business parks and forms a logical link towards the Nedersaksen line. It improves accessibility, supports employment, and strengthens regional connections.

125342.explanation: We are in favor of an independent investigation. Previous research used models and existing data but no local measurements. For clarity and trust among residents, independent measurements in Veendam itself are needed.

statements.title: Wildervank train station should be reopened.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The residents of Veendam should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Veendam moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Veendam should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: There should be an independent investigation into the lead levels in the air.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: High-rise residential apartment blocks should be allowed in Veendam.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Veendam must make agreements with the government about maximum ground subsidence resulting from salt extraction.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more CCTV surveillance in Veendam.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The center of Veendam should become car-free.
More business parks should be built in Veendam.
The municipality should collect waste more often in Veendam-North.
